class FenwickTree(size: Int) {
  private val tree = Array.fill(size + 1)(0)
  def update(index: Int, delta: Int): Unit = {
    var i = index + 1 // 1-based index
    while (i <= size) {
      tree(i) += delta
      i += i & -i
    }
  }
  def query(index: Int): Int = {
    var res = 0
    var i = index + 1 // 1-based index
    while (i > 0) {
      res += tree(i)
      i -= i & -i
    }
    res
  }
  def rangeQuery(l: Int, r: Int): Int = {
    if (l > r) 0
    else query(r) - query(l - 1)
  }
}

class FenwickTree(size: Int) {
  private val tree = Array.ofDim[Int](size + 1)
  def update(index: Int, delta: Int): Unit = {
    var i = index + 1
    while (i <= size) {
      tree(i) += delta
      i += i & -i
    }
  }
  def query(index: Int): Int = {
    var res = 0
    var i = index + 1
    while (i > 0) {
      res += tree(i)
      i -= i & -i
    }
    res
  }
  def rangeQuery(l: Int, r: Int): Int = {
    if (l > r) 0
    else query(r) - query(l - 1)
  }
}

def solve(nums: Array[Long], queries: Array[Array[Long]]): Array[Long] = {
  val n = nums.length
  val ft = Array.fill(6)(new FenwickTree(n))
  // Precompute depths
  val depths = Array.fill(n)(0)
  for (i <- 0 until n) {
    depths(i) = computeDepth(nums(i))
  }
  // Initialize Fenwick Trees
  for (i <- 0 until n) {
    val d = depths(i)
    ft(d).update(i, 1)
  }
  val answer = Array[Long]()
  for (q <- queries) {
    if (q(0) == 1) {
      val l = q(1).toInt
      val r = q(2).toInt
      val k = q(3).toInt
      val res = ft(k).rangeQuery(l, r)
      answer += res.toLong
    } else {
      val idx = q(1).toInt
      val val = q(2).toLong
      val oldD = depths(idx)
      ft(oldD).update(idx, -1)
      nums(idx) = val
      val newD = computeDepth(val)
      ft(newD).update(idx, 1)
      depths(idx) = newD
    }
  }

}
